verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum intravit Capharnaum post dies, et auditum est quod in domo esset,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν εἰσῆλθεν εἰς καπερναοὺμ δι᾽ ἡμερῶν καὶ ἠκούσθη ὅτι εἰς οἶκον ἐστίν
verses.king_james: And again he entered into Capernaum after *some* days; and it was noised that he was in the house.

verses.latin_vulgate: et convenerunt multi, ita ut non caperet neque ad januam, et loquebatur eis verbum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθὲως συνήχθησαν πολλοὶ ὥστε μηκέτι χωρεῖν μηδὲ τὰ πρὸς τὴν θύραν καὶ ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς τὸν λόγον
verses.king_james: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive *them*, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt ad eum ferentes paralyticum, qui a quatuor portabatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔρχονται πρὸς αὐτὸν παραλυτικὸν φέροντες αἰρόμενον ὑπὸ τεσσάρων
verses.king_james: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum non possent offerre eum illi præ turba, nudaverunt tectum ubi erat: et patefacientes submiserunt grabatum in quo paralyticus jacebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ δυνάμενοι προσεγγίσαι αὐτῷ διὰ τὸν ὄχλον ἀπεστέγασαν τὴν στέγην ὅπου ἦν καὶ ἐξορύξαντες χαλῶσιν τὸν κράββατον ἐφ᾽ ὧ ὁ παραλυτικὸς κατέκειτο
verses.king_james: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken *it* up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem vidisset Jesus fidem illorum, ait paralytico: Fili, dimittuntur tibi peccata tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς τὴν πίστιν αὐτῶν λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ τέκνον ἀφέωνται σοι αἱ ἁμαρτίαι σου
verses.king_james: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem illic quidam de scribis sedentes, et cogitantes in cordibus suis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δέ τινες τῶν γραμματέων ἐκεῖ καθήμενοι καὶ διαλογιζόμενοι ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid hic sic loquitur? blasphemat. Quis potest dimittere peccata, nisi solus Deus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὗτος οὕτως λαλεῖ βλασφημὶας τίς δύναται ἀφιέναι ἁμαρτίας εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: Why doth this *man* thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo statim cognito Jesus spiritu suo, quia sic cogitarent intra se, dicit illis: Quid ista cogitatis in cordibus vestris?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθὲως ἐπιγνοὺς ὁ ἰησοῦς τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ ὅτι οὕτως διαλογίζονται ἐν ἑαυτοῖς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς τί ταῦτα διαλογίζεσθε ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est facilius dicere paralytico: Dimittuntur tibi peccata tua: an dicere: Surge, tolle grabatum tuum, et ambula?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί ἐστιν εὐκοπώτερον εἰπεῖν τῷ παραλυτικῷ ἀφέωνταί σοί αἱ ἁμαρτίαι ἢ εἰπεῖν ἕγειραι καὶ ἆρον σου τὸν κράββατον καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, *Thy* sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut autem sciatis quia Filius hominis habet potestatem in terra dimittendi peccata (ait paralytico),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε ὅτι ἐξουσίαν ἔχει ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἀφιέναι ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἁμαρτίας λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ
verses.king_james: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)

verses.latin_vulgate: tibi dico: Surge, tolle grabatum tuum, et vade in domum tuam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σοὶ λέγω ἔγειραι καὶ ἆρον τὸν κράββατον σου καὶ ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου
verses.king_james: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim surrexit ille: et, sublato grabato, abiit coram omnibus, ita ut mirarentur omnes, et honorificent Deum, dicentes: Quia numquam sic vidimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠγέρθη εὐθὲως, καὶ ἄρας τὸν κράββατον ἐξῆλθεν ἐναντίον πάντων ὥστε ἐξίστασθαι πάντας καὶ δοξάζειν τὸν θεὸν λέγοντας ὅτι οὐδέποτε οὕτως εἴδομεν
verses.king_james: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressus est rursus ad mare, omnisque turba veniebat ad eum, et docebat eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν πάλιν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ἤρχετο πρὸς αὐτόν καὶ ἐδίδασκεν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum præteriret, vidit Levi Alphæi sedentem ad telonium, et ait illi: Sequere me. Et surgens secutus est eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παράγων εἶδεν λευὶν τὸν τοῦ ἁλφαίου καθήμενον ἐπὶ τὸ τελώνιον καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ἀκολούθει μοι καὶ ἀναστὰς ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the *son* of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, cum accumberet in domo illius, multi publicani et peccatores simul discumbebant cum Jesu et discipulis ejus: erant enim multi, qui et sequebantur eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγενέτο ἐν τῷ κατακεῖσθαι αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ αὐτοῦ καὶ πολλοὶ τελῶναι καὶ ἁμαρτωλοὶ συνανέκειντο τὦ ἰησοῦ καὶ τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ἦσαν γὰρ πολλοὶ καὶ ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scribæ et pharisæi videntes quia manducaret cum publicanis et peccatoribus, dicebant discipulis ejus: Quare cum publicanis et peccatoribus manducat et bibit Magister vester?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι, ἰδόντες αὐτὸν ἐσθίοντα μετὰ τῶν τελωνῶν καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν ἔλεγον τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ τι ὅτι μετὰ τῶν τελωνῶν καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν ἐσθίει καὶ πίνει
verses.king_james: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc audito Jesus ait illis: Non necesse habent sani medico, sed qui male habent: non enim veni vocare justos, sed peccatores.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀκούσας ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν οἱ ἰσχύοντες ἰατροῦ ἀλλ᾽ οἱ κακῶς ἔχοντες οὐκ ἦλθον καλέσαι δικαίους ἀλλὰ ἁμαρτωλούς εἰς μετάνοιαν
verses.king_james: When Jesus heard *it*, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erant discipuli Joannis et pharisæi jejunantes: et veniunt, et dicunt illi: Quare discipuli Joannis et pharisæorum jejunant, tui autem discipuli non jejunant?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦσαν οἱ μαθηταὶ ἰωάννου καὶ οἱ τῶν φαρισαίων νηστεύοντες καὶ ἔρχονται καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ διατί οἱ μαθηταὶ ἰωάννου καὶ οἱ τῶν φαρισαίων νηστεύουσιν οἱ δὲ σοὶ μαθηταὶ οὐ νηστεύουσιν
verses.king_james: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis Jesus: Numquid possunt filii nuptiarum, quamdiu sponsus cum illis est, jejunare? Quanto tempore habent secum sponsum, non possunt jejunare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς μὴ δύνανται οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος ἐν ᾧ ὁ νυμφίος μετ᾽ αὐτῶν ἐστιν νηστεύειν ὅσον χρόνον μεθ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ἔχουσιν τὸν νυμφίον οὐ δύνανται νηστεύειν
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venient autem dies cum auferetur ab eis sponsus: et tunc jejunabunt in illis diebus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλεύσονται δὲ ἡμέραι ὅταν ἀπαρθῇ ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν ὁ νυμφίος καὶ τότε νηστεύσουσιν ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραῖς
verses.king_james: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo assumentum panni rudis assuit vestimento veteri: alioquin aufert supplementum novum a veteri, et major scissura fit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐπίβλημα ῥάκους ἀγνάφου ἐπιῤῥάπτει ἐπὶ ἱμάτίῳ παλαιῷ εἰ δὲ μή αἴρει τὸ πλήρωμα αὐτοῦ τὸ καινὸν τοῦ παλαιοῦ και χεῖρον σχίσμα γίνεται
verses.king_james: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nemo mittit vinum novum in utres veteres: alioquin dirumpet vinum utres, et vinum effundetur, et utres peribunt: sed vinum novum in utres novos mitti debet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐδεὶς βάλλει οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς παλαιούς εἰ δὲ μή ῥήσσει ὁ οἶνος ὁ νέος τοὺς ἀσκούς καὶ ὁ οἶνος ἐκχεῖται, καὶ οἱ ἀσκοί ἀπόλοῦνται ἀλλὰ οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς καινούς βλητέον
verses.king_james: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est iterum Dominus sabbatis ambularet per sata, et discipuli ejus cœperunt progredi, et vellere spicas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο παραπορεύεσθαι αὐτὸν ἐν τοῖς σάββασιν διὰ τῶν σπορίμων καὶ ἤρξαντο οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ὁδὸν ποιεῖν τίλλοντες τοὺς στάχυας
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæi autem dicebant ei: Ecce, quid faciunt sabbatis quod non licet?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι ἔλεγον αὐτῷ Ἴδε τί ποιοῦσιν ἐν τοῖς σάββασιν ὃ οὐκ ἔξεστιν
verses.king_james: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Numquam legistis quid fecerit David, quando necessitatem habuit, et esuriit ipse, et qui cum eo erant?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς οὐδέποτε ἀνέγνωτε τί ἐποίησεν δαβίδ, ὅτε χρείαν ἔσχεν καὶ ἐπείνασεν αὐτός καὶ οἱ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?

verses.latin_vulgate: quomodo introibit in domum Dei sub Abiathar principe sacerdotum, et panes propositionis manducavit, quos non licebat manducare, nisi sacerdotibus, et dedit eis qui cum eo erant?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πῶς εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ ἀβιαθὰρ τοῦ ἀρχιερέως καὶ τοὺς ἄρτους τῆς προθέσεως ἔφαγεν οὓς οὐκ ἔξεστιν φαγεῖν εἰ μὴ τοῖς ἱερεῦσιν καὶ ἔδωκεν καὶ τοῖς σὺν αὐτῷ οὖσιν
verses.king_james: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicebat eis: Sabbatum propter hominem factum est, et non homo propter sabbatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς τὸ σάββατον διὰ τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἐγένετο οὐχ ὁ ἄνθρωπος διὰ τὸ σάββατον
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque Dominus est Filius hominis, etiam sabbati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε κύριός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου καὶ τοῦ σαββάτου
verses.king_james: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
